focus: Inventory automated quality machinery and coverage gaps across the spec-kit surface

label: All 33 validator-registry rules are spec-doc-scoped; none target skill docs, references, command docs, or context-engineering assets
validate.sh is a completion-time manual gate, not wired into the commit hook even for spec docs
The active pre-commit hook gates code/MCP/prompt-registry integrity (5 gates) but never document data-quality, DQI, or retrieval-shaping frontmatter
sk-doc extract_structure.py computes a deterministic type-aware DQI (0-100) with per-type gates but is never automated on-write or retroactively
Skill/reference/command docs carry trigger_phrases/importance_tier/contextType (floor-bypassing write-time retrieval+adherence signals) with zero automated quality gate
generate-context post-save-review.ts auto-reviews trigger_phrase quality but only on spec-folder memory saves, fixes hand-applied
Write-time/adherence/logic signals bypass the 3-result prod truncation floor; retrieval signals pay it
The most mature quality gate (validate.sh, 33 rules) is structurally blind to the largest doc surface (skills+references)

direction: A corpus-wide retroactive doc-quality sweep already exists
reason: validation.md batch snippets are illustrative shell examples, not a shipped runner; no wiring of DQI across the surface found
